company.what_they_do: ML inference serving platform — deploys, scales, and monitors models as production APIs

problems.title: Cold-start latency for large model containers on serverless GPU infrastructure
problems.description: Baseten serves ML models through a serverless GPU architecture. Loading large model weights (10GB+) from cold storage on first invocation causes multi-minute latency, which breaks real-time inference SLAs and discourages latency-sensitive use cases. This is a well-documented challenge for serverless ML platforms.

problems.title: GPU memory fragmentation across concurrent model deployments
problems.description: When multiple model versions or tenants share GPU memory, allocation fragmentation reduces throughput and increases OOM failures. This is a systemic issue in multi-tenant ML serving.

people.name: Tero Karras
people.relevance_reason: Leads engineering efforts on the core inference serving stack

people.name: Bola Malek
people.relevance_reason: CEO — sets technical direction for the platform

packet.technical_note:
## Technical Deep-Dive: Cold-Start Optimization for Baseten

### Problem
Baseten's serverless GPU architecture loads model weights from cloud storage on first invocation. For large models (LLMs, diffusion models), this cold start can take several minutes.

### Build Plan
1. Implement model weight prefetching using a warm-pool strategy that keeps frequently-used model shards in GPU memory across invocations.
2. Use incremental loading with priority-based weight ordering — load critical layers first, defer non-essential parameters.
3. Evaluate model quantization (FP16/INT8) to reduce memory footprint for cold-start paths.

### Success Criteria
- Cold-start latency under 5 seconds for models up to 7B parameters
- No regression in steady-state inference throughput
- Memory overhead from warm pools stays under 20% of available GPU memory

### Adjacent Work
https://github.com/basetenlabs/truss — the open-source model serving framework. Understanding its container boot sequence is the first step.

outreach_drafts.email:
Hi Tero

I've been looking at cold-start latency in serverless GPU inference and noticed Baseten's architecture loads full model weights from cloud storage on each new invocation. For large models this can take minutes under load.

I have experience with warm-pool prefetching strategies — I worked on a similar approach for PyTorch model serving that cut cold starts from 90s to under 3s. Happy to share the approach if it's useful.

Best,
UpSearch User
outreach_drafts.linkedin_note: Love the work on Baseten's inference stack. I've been thinking about cold-start optimizations for serverless GPU — would be great to compare notes.
outreach_drafts.connection_followup: Following up on the cold-start work. Happy to share details if useful.
